description: Figure 5 presents the X-ray diffraction patterns of Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni low-density steel samples fabricated by selective laser melting (SLM) at five different laser powers: P-70, P-90, P-110, P-130, and P-150. All five curves display diffraction peaks indexed exclusively to the austenite phase γ-Fe(M) with visible reflections at (111), (200), (220), (311), and (222), and the legend confirms the phase identification. Notably, no α-Fe(M) phase is detected in any of the SLM-printed samples, unlike the precursor powder. The caption and accompanying text report that the full width at half maximum (FWHM) of the (111) peak systematically decreases from 0.289 for P-70 to 0.237 for P-150, indicating a gradual increase in average grain size as the laser energy density rises. This figure supports the core conclusion that SLM processing with varying laser power produces a fully austenitic microstructure while enabling grain size control through energy density adjustment.
